task_description: A small CRUD REST endpoint for user records with in-memory storage
prompt: Implement a complete in-memory CRUD REST API for user records. Users have id, name, email, and createdAt. Support: GET /users (list all), GET /users/:id (get one), POST /users (create), PUT /users/:id (update), DELETE /users/:id (delete). Return 404 for missing users. Generate sequential IDs.

emission_blocks.description: define the User entity type and an in-memory store for user records
create a new user record in the in-memory store returning the created user
retrieve a user by numeric id from the store returning null when not found
list all users in the store sorted by id ascending
update an existing user record by id returning the updated user or null when not found
delete a user from the store by id returning true when deleted false when not found

task_description: A CLI subcommand that lists and filters items from a JSON file
prompt: Implement a CLI subcommand `list` that reads a JSON file of items (each with name, tags[], and active boolean), filters by --tag and --active flags, sorts by name, and prints a table to stdout. If the file doesn't exist, print an error and exit 1.

emission_blocks.description: parse CLI arguments for a list command supporting tag filter and active-only filter options
read and parse a JSON file returning parsed content or throwing a descriptive error if missing or malformed
filter a list of items by tag and active status and sort by name field
format an array of items as a fixed-width text table with name tags and active columns
write a string to process stdout handling EPIPE errors gracefully

task_description: A typed event emitter with subscribe, unsubscribe, and emit operations
prompt: Implement a generic typed event emitter class. Support multiple listeners per event, listener removal by reference, once() for single-fire listeners, and emit() that calls all listeners synchronously. No external dependencies.

emission_blocks.description: implement a typed event emitter with on off once and emit methods
check whether a number is within a half-open interval returning true when min is inclusive and max is exclusive
deep-clone a plain JSON-serializable object value using JSON round-trip
